message:
fix(api): replace optional-chaining .trim() with explicit typeof guards

Across all API route handlers, payload fields were accessed via
optional chaining (e.g. payload.field?.trim()) which silently calls
.trim() on any truthy value, including numbers or booleans, producing
unexpected results or runtime errors.

Replaced with explicit typeof === "string" guards so non-string inputs
are treated as empty/missing rather than coerced. This prevents type
confusion attacks where a numeric payload field could bypass validation
that expects a string format (e.g. a numeric actionId passing a
.trim() call but failing downstream lookups silently).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
